title: Agent Audit — Argo vs Hermes vs Goose (+ Claude Code)
status: v0 — Argo/ClaudeCode/local-Hermes populated; Hermes & Goose deep-dives enriching

vision.statement: Argo's sharpened product vision: a trusted personal AI operator FOR neurodivergent people — built to support executive function and other ND traits (broad ND: dyslexia, dyscalculia, autism, ADHD, aphantasia, and more — not just autism+ADHD). This sharpens the manifesto's 'trusted digital operator' with a who and a why. Grounded in Argo's existing neurodivergent-first brain identity and the operator's own profile. The kernel-safety thesis (safe by construction) is itself an ND feature: it removes the vigilance burden.

filters.name: Initiation friction
filters.desc: Reduces task activation cost — lowers the 'getting started' barrier (the core ADHD/exec-function wall).

filters.name: Externalized memory
filters.desc: Offloads working memory — remembers goals, context, where you left off, what was decided.

filters.name: Calm output
filters.desc: Low-arousal, low-firehose, sensory-regulating output — never a wall of noise.

filters.name: Low decision load
filters.desc: Sensible defaults; doesn't demand constant micro-choices (decision fatigue / analysis paralysis).

filters.name: Structure & follow-through
filters.desc: Breaks down, sequences, nudges, tracks — supports planning and follow-through deficits.

filters.name: Reliability / trust
filters.desc: Predictable, safe, honest — removes the vigilance burden. Safe-by-construction = ND feature.

vision.curationBias: RUTHLESS by design. Six escalating 'add to goal' messages is itself scope creep — so this audit's job is a SMALL curated pull-set and a LOT of explicit DROP. A feature being slick in a dev-tool (Goose) or broad in Hermes does not make it fit an ND executive-function assistant. Default verdict is DROP unless it scores ≥2 ND filters with low cognitive cost.

agents.name: Argo
agents.tagline: Trusted operator: knows the goal, enforces scope on every action, reports only verified output.

agents.name: Hermes (upstream)
agents.tagline: The self-improving AI agent — built-in learning loop, skills from experience, runs on a $5 VPS, text it from Telegram.
agents.vsArgo: Same lineage/concept — Argo hardens Hermes's breadth into a kernel-enforced safety contract. Heavy overlap expected; delta is what matters.

agents.name: Hermes (local customized)
agents.tagline: Jason's customized Hermes — the bar Argo set out to match then exceed.
agents.vsArgo: Customizations: gateway streaming dispatch, ai-gateway provider plugin, browser_providers, telegram/cron tweaks.

agents.name: Goose
agents.tagline: Native open-source AI agent — desktop, CLI, API — for code & workflows.
agents.vsArgo: Different lineage (Block). Dev-tool-centric → expect many DROPs, a few genuinely novel ideas (extensions/MCP maturity, recipes, scheduler).

agents.name: Claude Code (reference)
agents.tagline: The reference harness — surveyed for plumbing/permission/runnability patterns.
agents.vsArgo: Study-only; reimplement clean. Findings are infra-level (permission protocol, capability matrix, activity-feed extraction).

argoInventory.note: Argo's current surface — the dedupe baseline. 675 tests green (648 TS + 27 Rust).

categories.name: Architecture
items: Rust safety kernel (assess() gates every tool call: Allow/Ask/Block)
Approvals queue + goal ledger + event log
TS agent layer (Node22/ESM/tsx, no build step)
Kernel is the boundary — TS cannot bypass it

categories.name: TUI / UX
items: React/Ink streaming transcript + tool activity + spinner
Inline approvals, slash commands, custom composer
TUI-SMOOTH: clean per-tool activity feed (no JSON/temp-path firehose)

categories.name: Prompt
items: Skill index injected; recall loads full body

categories.name: Self-improvement
items: Background review → writes skills tagged argo-learned
Safe curator (archives only stale learned skills, never deletes)

categories.name: Autonomy / reach
items: MCP: consume (mount servers) + serve (Argo as MCP server)
Cron schedule, project rooms, operator modes, model routing

ndVisionAlignment.note: How Argo's EXISTING features already serve the ND vision — and where the gaps are.

strong.why: Removes vigilance burden — the operator never has to police the agent; the boundary is physical.
Externalizes goals and context across sessions — offloads working memory; supports follow-through.
Builds a persistent model of the user; the ND framing is already encoded.
Just shipped — kills the firehose; low-arousal output.

gaps.note: Candidate pull target — the activation wall is the #1 ND exec-function need.
Candidate — presence + nudges support initiation & follow-through.
Candidate — broad ND, not just exec function.

comparison.note: Decision-level capability matrix. Cells: ✓ has · ~ partial · ✗ lacks · — n/a. Hermes/Goose cells refined by deep-dive agents.

rows.note: Argo's structural differentiator — safe by construction, not by suggestion.
Low-friction capture from anywhere = initiation aid.
Pending Goose deep-dive — depth of extension UX.
Prevents 400s on free-typed OpenRouter/Ollama models → reliability.

pulls.note: WORTH ADDING — curated, ND-aligned. Small by design.

items.why: The activation wall is the central ND exec-function need; nothing in any of the 3 agents targets it directly. This is Argo's differentiator, not a copy.
Free-typed OpenRouter/Ollama models get sent thinking/effort params they 400 on. Small declarative map fixes a real reliability gap.
Reinforces TUI-SMOOTH — bounded, summarized activity. Already partly done; pattern confirms direction.

pulls.pendingFrom: Hermes & Goose deep-dive agents will add their curated PULLs here.

drops.note: LOSE TOTALLY / DO NOT BUILD — fails the ND filter or duplicates Argo.

items.why: Argo already has 7 providers incl OpenRouter (the same 'one key, many models' value). Duplicate; adds cognitive surface.
Argo already has Playwright browser tools. Likely duplicate — verify, then drop.
Platform-thinking before users; adds choice load (F4 violation). Anti-ND.

drops.pendingFrom: Hermes & Goose deep-dive agents will add the bulk of DROPs here (esp. Goose dev-power-tooling).

issues.note: GitHub issue triage — worth-adding vs not, ND-lens. Populated by deep-dive agents.

localHermesDelta.note: Customizations in ~/.hermes/hermes-agent vs the clean baseline — what Jason+Claude changed.

items.what: Streaming events through the gateway (live dispatch + event stream).
items.why: Check if Argo's gateway already streams; if not, this aids calm live feedback (F3).

items.what: Telegram draft formatting + cronjob tool adjustments.
items.why: Minor polish; revisit only if Argo's telegram/cron output is noisy (F3).

claudeCode.note: Claude Code CLI survey (leaked-claude-code = partial leak/plumbing layer; claude-code-haha = runnability layer). Study-only; reimplement clean.

items.note: → PULL. Real Argo gap: free-typed models 400 on unsupported params.
Decouples Ask-resolution from the kernel gate; ports cleanly. Argo has no hooks layer.

items.finding: Hooks race the UI prompt (first wins) via stream-json control protocol
items.note: Programmatic policy auto-decides without blocking the user — could reduce approval fatigue.

items.finding: Recovery/degraded readline CLI behind a flag
items.note: Resilience when Ink can't render (CI/headless/native-dep failure).
Gateway event-delivery robustness — verify vs Argo's gateway first.
Optional native deps should degrade, never break the input loop.
ANTI-FINDING — do not lift the entrypoint to Node/tsx without stubbing.

items.finding: Not in leak: system prompt, compaction, todo UX, grep/glob/multiedit, thinking, prompt-caching
items.note: Partial leak — cannot source these. Honest gap.
